Protein families: Class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily, Protein arginine N-methyltransferase family
Sequence similarities: SIMILARITY: Belongs to the class I-like SAM-binding methyltransferase superfamily. Protein arginine N-methyltransferase family. {ECO:0000255|PROSITE-ProRule:PRU01015}.
Catalytic activity: CATALYTIC ACTIVITY: Reaction=L-arginine + S-adenosyl-L-methionine = N(omega)-methyl-L-arginine + S-adenosyl-L-homocysteine + H(+); Xref=Rhea:RHEA:59756, ChEBI:CHEBI:15378, ChEBI:CHEBI:32682, ChEBI:CHEBI:57856, ChEBI:CHEBI:59789, ChEBI:CHEBI:114953; EC=2.1.1.381; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:30728519, ECO:0000269|PubMed:30763082}; PhysiologicalDirection=left-to-right; Xref=Rhea:RHEA:59757; Evidence={ECO:0000269|PubMed:30728519, ECO:0000269|PubMed:30763082};
Function [CC]: FUNCTION: Involved in the biosynthesis of the glucosamine-nitrosourea antibiotic streptozotocin (SZN) (PubMed:30728519, PubMed:30763082). Catalyzes the conversion of L-arginine to N(omega)-methyl-L-arginine (L-NMA), using S-adenosyl-L-methionine (SAM) as a methyl donor (PubMed:30728519, PubMed:30763082). {ECO:0000269|PubMed:30728519, ECO:0000269|PubMed:30763082}.
